risks.statement: High-value outbound wires may be released without independent dual authorization, causing financial loss and settlement error.

controls.description: Payment system enforces dual authorization on wires above USD 10,000; second approver cannot be the initiator.

evidence.result: 40/40 sampled wires had a distinct second approver; 0 exceptions

risks.statement: Sanctions screening of payment beneficiaries may fail to interdict a hit before release.

loss_events.description: Screening queue backlog delayed one release review beyond SLA (no true hit)

controls.description: Real-time sanctions screening on beneficiary and originator with mandatory analyst clearance of every alert before release.

evidence.result: 6/50 alerts cleared after SLA due to queue backlog; no missed true hit

remediation.action: Add screening-queue staffing during peak windows and an SLA-breach escalation trigger.

risks.statement: Reconciliation of the nostro settlement account may miss a break, delaying detection of a misdirected payment.

controls.description: Daily automated nostro reconciliation with break investigation and sign-off.

risks.statement: Manual fee overrides in the payment system may be applied without secondary review, causing revenue leakage.
Payment operations staff access may exceed least privilege following a role change.

controls.description: Quarterly user-access recertification with line-manager approval and revocation on failure.

evidence.result: 100% of 38 users recertified; 3 excess grants revoked
